questions.question: When you join a workspace on the Plus, Business, or Enterprise Plan, how are default teamspaces handled?

options.text: You must manually request access to each default teamspace.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article states that you are automatically added to any teamspaces that have been set as default when you join the workspace.

options.text: You are automatically added to any teamspaces that have been set as default.
options.explanation: Correct. This is the standard behavior for Plus, Business, and Enterprise plans when you join a workspace.

options.text: Default teamspace access is only granted to workspace owners.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article mentions that default teamspaces are added automatically for all members who join the workspace.

options.text: Default teamspaces are not available on these plans.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article explicitly lists Plus, Business, and Enterprise as the plans that allow joining workspaces.

questions.question: Which plans allow access to the private teamspace feature?

options.text: Plus Plan only
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article specifies that Workspaces on the Business Plan or Enterprise Plan have access to the private teamspace feature.

options.text: Business Plan or Enterprise Plan
options.explanation: Correct. Only workspaces on the Business or Enterprise plans can utilize the private teamspace feature.

options.text: Free Plan
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article focuses on Plus, Business, and Enterprise plans; the Free plan is not mentioned regarding this feature.

options.text: All paid plans
options.explanation: Incorrect. While Plus, Business, and Enterprise are mentioned as joining workspaces, only Business and Enterprise are specified as having access to private teamspaces.

questions.question: To view all existing teamspaces in your workspace, where should you navigate?

options.text: Click the Teamspace label in the sidebar
options.explanation: Incorrect. You need to click the 'More' (•••) option in the left sidebar to access the full list of teamspaces.

options.text: In your left sidebar, click ••• More
options.explanation: Correct. Following step 1 in the article, you click 'More' in the sidebar to reveal the list of teamspaces you are part of and all open/closed teamspaces.

options.text: Go to Settings > Teamspaces
options.explanation: Incorrect. While you can check settings there, the instructions for *browsing* the list point to the sidebar.

options.text: Click the 'Request' button on a closed teamspace
options.explanation: Incorrect. You must access the list from the sidebar before you can see which teamspaces are closed and available for request.

questions.question: How can you add a new member to a teamspace you have created?

options.text: Click the + button next to the Teamspaces label
options.explanation: Incorrect. The '+' button is used to create a new teamspace, not to add members to an existing one.

options.text: Hover over the teamspace name, click •••, and choose Teamspace settings
options.explanation: Correct. The article outlines the steps: hover over the name, click the menu, select 'Teamspace settings', and then 'Add members'.

options.text: Duplicate the existing teamspace
options.explanation: Incorrect. Duplicating a teamspace creates a copy of it, rather than adding a new member to the existing one.

options.text: Send an email invitation from the workspace settings
options.explanation: Incorrect. Invitations must be sent specifically from within the Teamspace settings menu after hovering over the teamspace name.

questions.question: What is the correct procedure to customize a member's role within a teamspace?

options.text: Click the member's name directly in the member list
options.explanation: Incorrect. There is no direct 'click name' option in the UI described; you must use the dropdown menu.

options.text: Click the dropdown to the right of their name in Teamspace settings
options.explanation: Correct. The article states that you can 'customize their role by clicking the dropdown to the right of their name'.

options.text: Ask the member to update their own role
options.explanation: Incorrect. Only the owner or members with administrative rights can customize the roles of others.

options.text: The role is automatically customized based on the user's email domain
options.explanation: Incorrect. Roles are manually customized by the owner via the dropdown menu, not automatically.

questions.question: If you want to duplicate an existing teamspace, where do you find that option?

options.text: In the 'More' menu in the sidebar
options.explanation: Incorrect. The 'More' menu is used to view teamspaces; duplication is done from the specific teamspace options.

options.text: Click the ••• next to a teamspace and select Duplicate teamspace
options.explanation: Correct. The article explicitly states: 'To duplicate an existing teamspace, click the ••• next to a teamspace and click Duplicate teamspace.'

options.text: In the workspace Settings under Teamspaces
options.explanation: Incorrect. While you can check settings there, the specific action to duplicate is found by clicking the menu next to the teamspace name in the sidebar.

options.text: There is no option to duplicate a teamspace
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article provides specific instructions on how to duplicate a teamspace.

questions.question: What distinguishes an 'Open' teamspace from a 'Closed' teamspace in the browse menu?

options.text: Open teamspaces require a request, while closed teamspaces allow direct joining
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article states the opposite: 'Open teamspaces can be joined directly' and 'Closed teamspaces will say close. Click Request'.

options.text: Open teamspaces can be joined directly, while closed teamspaces require a request
options.explanation: Correct. This is the key distinction described in the 'Join or leave teamspaces' section.

options.text: Closed teamspaces are visible to everyone, while open teamspaces are private
options.explanation: Incorrect. The visibility of closed teamspaces depends on the specific permissions (like private teamspace features), but the general distinction is the access method (Request vs. Join).

options.text: Open teamspaces are only for owners, and closed teamspaces are for members
options.explanation: Incorrect. Both states have specific instructions for joining, but the mechanism differs (Direct join vs. Request).

questions.question: What restriction might prevent you from creating a new teamspace?

options.text: You must be a member of the workspace, not just an owner
options.explanation: Incorrect. The restriction mentioned in the text applies to owners specifically, not preventing them from creating teamspaces.

options.text: Depending on workspace settings, the action may be restricted to workspace owners only
options.explanation: Correct. The article notes that restrictions can be applied at the workspace level, limiting the creation of new teamspaces to owners.

options.text: You cannot create a teamspace if you are on the Plus Plan
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article lists Plus as a plan where you can join workspaces and create teamspaces (assuming no specific workspace restrictions).

options.text: It is impossible to create a teamspace if you are already in one
options.explanation: Incorrect. You can create additional teamspaces even if you are already a member of existing ones.

questions.question: When you create a new teamspace, what role do you automatically assume?

options.text: Member
options.explanation: Incorrect. By default, the creator of a teamspace assumes the highest level of authority within that specific teamspace.

options.text: Admin
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article specifies the exact title of the role.

options.text: Owner
options.explanation: Correct. The article states, 'When you create a teamspace, you will become the owner of this teamspace by default.'

options.text: Guest
options.explanation: Incorrect. A guest typically has limited access. The creator is the primary authority.

questions.question: How do you leave a teamspace you are currently a member of?

options.text: Go to the Teamspace settings and click Leave
options.explanation: Incorrect. While you go to settings, you must access it via the hover menu over the teamspace name, not a general 'Leave' button in settings.

options.text: Hover over the teamspace name in your sidebar, click •••, and choose Leave teamspace
options.explanation: Correct. The article lists these exact steps to leave a teamspace.

options.text: Click the 'Leave' button in the main workspace sidebar
options.explanation: Incorrect. The action is specific to the teamspace menu, not the general workspace sidebar.

options.text: You cannot leave a teamspace you created
options.explanation: Incorrect. You can leave a teamspace you created; the instructions apply to any member currently in the teamspace.
